aliases: Korean Spicy Rice Cakes（韩式辣炒年糕）

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tteokbokki; 'rice cake food product' is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Rice-based dishes) used as the closest category match; no tteokbokki-specific FoodEx2 code exists in the published hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tteokbokki or Korean spicy rice cakes.

allergens: gluten (wheat in gochujang and fish cakes)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of tteokbokki is approximately 250 g, equivalent to one standard street-food or restaurant bowl containing roughly 8–10 rice cake cylinders with sauce and fish cakes.

nutrient_source.title: Tteokbokki (Korean Spicy Rice Cakes) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at 0.95 confidence for all per-100g nutrient fields. Values preserved as supplied. Saturated fat (0.8 g/100g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central entry for tteokbokki (NDB No. closest match: Korean rice cake dish) to supplement the HPB record which does not report saturated fat separately.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice cake and refined rice starch products
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for tteokbokki exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 82 is estimated from the closest analogous food: plain rice cakes (garaetteok / puffed rice cake products) which carry a GI of 78–87 in the Sydney GI Database (mean ~82). Gochujang sauce and dietary fibre (3.7 g/100g) may modestly attenuate glycaemic response, but the dominant substrate is refined rice starch; GI 82 is therefore a conservative defensible estimate. GL per serving = (82 × 25.19 g carbohydrate × 250 g serving) / (100 × 100) = 51.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100g; a 250 g serving delivers ~578 mg sodium (~25% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), partially offset by potassium 327 mg/100g.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit tteokbokki to a half-serving (≤125 g) and request reduced gochujang and soy sauce to keep the meal's sodium contribution below 400 mg; avoid adding extra condiments at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~82) from refined rice starch; a 250 g serving delivers ~51.5 GL units, which is high and will cause a rapid postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict tteokbokki to a small portion (≤100 g), pair it with non-starchy vegetables and a protein source to blunt the glycaemic response, and monitor blood glucose 1–2 hours post-meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100g and moderate fat 4.21 g/100g; saturated fat is low at ~0.8 g/100g and cholesterol is 0 mg/100g, which are favourable; however, sodium load at full serving is a concern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular health, limit tteokbokki to a small portion (≤150 g), choose preparations with less gochujang and soy sauce to reduce sodium, and balance the meal with potassium-rich vegetables to support vascular function.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (gochujang, gochugaru) contain solanine-related alkaloids that may aggravate inflammation in nightshade-sensitive RA individuals; fibre 3.7 g/100g supports gut immune modulation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients with known nightshade sensitivity should request reduced or omitted chili paste; those without nightshade sensitivity may consume moderate portions as the anti-inflammatory polyphenols in garlic and sesame oil provide some benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tteokbokki (떡볶이), also romanized as ddeokbokki or topokki, is a beloved Korean street food and comfort dish consisting of chewy cylindrical rice cakes (garaetteok) simmered and stir-fried in a bold, fiery sauce made primarily from gochujang (Korean fermented red chili paste), gochugaru (red chili flakes), soy sauce, sugar, and garlic. The dish typically includes fish cakes (eomuk), green onions, sesame oil, and sesame seeds, and is finished with a glossy, sticky, intensely spiced sauce. The texture of the rice cakes is distinctively chewy and slightly elastic, contrasting with the soft fish cakes and the rich, umami-laden sauce. Tteokbokki originated as a royal court dish in the Joseon Dynasty but evolved into its modern spicy form in the 1950s and is now one of South Korea's most iconic street foods, widely available at pojangmacha (street stalls) and in Korean restaurants globally, including Singapore. Per 100 g, tteokbokki provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, 25.19 g carbohydrate, 3.7 g dietary fibre, and 231 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is high (~82) due to the refined rice starch base, making portion control critical for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate and warrants attention for those managing hypertension. The dish is dairy-free and contains allergens including gluten (from gochujang and fish cakes), fish, soy, and sesame.

confidence_reason: All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at 0.95 confidence. Saturated fat supplemented from USDA FoodData Central closest match. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous rice cake entries. Korean local-language name (떡볶이) is well-established and verified.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for tteokbokki not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source for this dish. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data not available in the HPB Foodo record for this dish and not reliably available in USDA FoodData Central for the composite tteokbokki preparation as of 2026-04-16. 4. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero trans fat per HPB source and consistent with the dish's ingredient profile (no hydrogenated fats). 5. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB source; fish cakes may contribute trace cholesterol but the HPB composite record reports 0.0. 6. Input record incorrectly listed primary_cuisine as 'Japanese'; corrected to 'Korean' as tteokbokki is a Korean dish. 7. GI of 82 is an analogous estimate based on refined rice cake products in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct tteokbokki GI measurement found in approved sources. 8. Saturated fat of 0.8 g/100g is adapted from USDA FoodData Central closest match for Korean rice cake dish; HPB record does not separately report saturated fat.
